name: American Red CrossHQ
mission: Since its founding in 1881 by visionary leader Clara Barton, the American Red Cross has been the nation's premier emergency response organization. We bring shelter, food and comfort to those affected by disasters, large and small. We collect lifesaving donated blood and supply it to patients in need. We provide support to our men and women in military bases around the world, and to the families they leave behind. We train communities in CPR, first aid and other skills that save lives. And we assist our neighbors abroad with critical disaster response, preparedness and disease prevention efforts. We are able to do all this by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors.
theoryOfChange: Mobilizing a volunteer force and donor base to provide immediate relief and lifesaving education to prevent and alleviate suffering.
scoreSummary: American Red CrossHQ shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause urgency is high due to its humanitarian mission, the alignment score of 31/50 reflects a medium donor fit for specialized charitable segments [1][3]. The organization is recognized as SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE, offering a reliable pathway for general charitable giving [12].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, maintaining a program expense ratio of 90.8% [1][2]. Its financial health is robust, evidenced by a fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised and a total revenue of $3,804,377,344 [2][8].

Strong liquidity with 12.2 months of working capital available

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the massive scale of the Red Cross translates into measurable relief for disaster victims.

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="1">perfect 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> across all four beacons, including finance and leadership.
The charity maintains a <cite id="1">90.8% program expense ratio</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="1">90.3% peer median</cite> for humanitarian groups.
With <cite id="1">12.2 months of working capital</cite>, the Red Cross has the financial depth to sustain long-term relief efforts.

strengths_deep_dive: The American Red Cross demonstrates institutional-grade governance with a <cite id="1">92% independent board</cite>. This oversight supports a highly efficient fundraising model that spends only <cite id="1">$0.06 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond disaster response, the organization is a primary provider of <cite id="7">biomedical products and services</cite>, generating significant program revenue to sustain its mission. Its <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> further confirms its commitment to public reporting and outcome tracking.
rich_narrative.summary: The American Red Cross is a massive humanitarian institution that <cite id="3">prevents and alleviates human suffering</cite> during emergencies. Since its <cite id="3">founding in 1881</cite>, it has become a cornerstone of the global relief network. The organization reported <cite id="2">$3,217,077,611 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. Its operations are vast, covering <cite id="13">disaster relief, blood services, and training</cite>. The charity maintains <cite id="1">a perfect 100/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting elite standards of accountability. While it operates internationally, it has a <cite id="8">high domestic focus</cite>, with significant resources staying within the United States. For donors, the Red Cross offers a reliable, large-scale vehicle for humanitarian aid. It manages <cite id="1">12.2 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring it can respond instantly to crises. The organization is <cite id="12">Sadaqah-eligible</cite> and provides a critical safety net for millions of people annually.
